perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement: (i) the term " Receiving Party " means the Party receiving Evaluation Material; (ii) the term " Furnishing Party " means the Party providing Evaluation Material or causing Evaluation Material to be provided; (iii) the term " Representatives " means the directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, investment bankers, financial advisors and other consultants and advisors engaged in connection with the review and evaluation of the Transaction) of the specified Party; and (iv) the term " Evaluation Material " means all proprietary and confidential information concerning the Furnishing Party or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates, whether in verbal, visual, written, electronic or other form, which is made available by the Furnishing Party or any of its Representatives to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives (" Primary Evaluation Material "), together, in each case, with all notes, memoranda, summaries, analyses, studies, compilations and other writings relating thereto or based in whole or in part thereon prepared by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives (" Derivative Evaluation Material ").
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement: (i) the term " Receiving Party " means the Party receiving Evaluation Material; (ii) the term " Furnishing Party " means the Party providing Evaluation Material or causing Evaluation Material to be provided; (iii) the term " Representatives " means the directors, officers, employees or agents of the specified Party; and (iv) the term " Evaluation Material " means all proprietary and confidential information concerning the Furnishing Party or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates, whether in verbal, visual, written, electronic or other form, which is made available by the Furnishing Party or any of its Representatives to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives (" Primary Evaluation Material "), together, in each case, with all notes, memoranda, summaries, analyses, studies, compilations and other writings relating thereto or based in whole or in part thereon prepared by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives (" Derivative Evaluation Material ").
perturbation.explanation: By removing "advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, investment bankers, financial advisors and other consultants and advisors engaged in connection with the review and evaluation of the Transaction)" from the definition of Representatives, it creates uncertainty because Section 3(a) states Evaluation Material shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required. With the removal, it's unclear if advisors are still considered 'Representatives' authorized to receive the information. This creates a direct contradiction since Section 3(a) implicitly assumes that 'Representatives' can include advisors, but the modified definition excludes them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, investment bankers, financial advisors and other consultants and advisors engaged in connection with the review and evaluation of the Transaction)' from the definition of 'Representatives.' The contradicted text states that Evaluation Material shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required. Since advisors are no longer explicitly included in the definition of Representatives, it creates a direct conflict and uncertainty as to whether the Receiving Party can disclose Evaluation Material to its advisors, contradicting the section about restricting dissemination of Evaluation Material to Representatives.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the term " Evaluation Material " shall not include, and the Parties' obligations herein (other than their obligations under paragraph 6 of this Agreement) shall not extend to, information which (a) was rightfully in the possession of the Receiving Party prior to disclosure by the Furnishing Party; (b) was or is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of the Evaluation Material; (c) is now or hereafter becomes available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party's Representatives in violation of this Agreement; (d) becomes available to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Furnishing Party or any of its Representatives and such source is not, to the knowledge of the Receiving Party following reasonable inquiry, under any obligation to the Furnishing Party or any of its Representatives (whether contractual, legal or fiduciary) to keep such information confidential; or (e) is transmitted by or on behalf of the Furnishing Party after receiving written notification from the Receiving Party of the termination of discussions relating to the Transaction or written instructions from the Receiving Party not to furnish any further Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the term " Evaluation Material " shall not include, and the Parties' obligations herein (other than their obligations under paragraph 6 of this Agreement) shall not extend to, information which (a) was rightfully in the possession of the Receiving Party prior to disclosure by the Furnishing Party; (b) was or is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of the Evaluation Material; (c) is now or hereafter becomes available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party or any of the Receiving Party's Representatives in violation of this Agreement; (d) becomes available to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Furnishing Party or any of its Representatives and such source is not, to the knowledge of the Receiving Party following reasonable inquiry, under any obligation to the Furnishing Party to keep such information confidential; or (e) is transmitted by or on behalf of the Furnishing Party after receiving written notification from the Receiving Party of the termination of discussions relating to the Transaction or written instructions from the Receiving Party not to furnish any further Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or any of its Representatives' from section 2(d), it creates a contradiction. The Receiving Party could argue that information received from a source under obligation to *the Furnishing Party's Representative* is not Evaluation Material, even though the source is indirectly bound to the Furnishing Party. Section 3(a) requires the Receiving Party to be responsible for their Representatives' breaches. If a Representative of the Furnishing party disclose info, is that still considered Evaluation Material?
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'or any of its Representatives' in section 2(d), which aligns with Section 3(a) by ensuring that information received from a source under obligation to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives is considered Evaluation Material. However, the changed text removes 'or any of its Representatives' from section 2(d). This creates a contradiction because the Receiving Party could argue that information received from a source under obligation to the Furnishing Party's Representative is not Evaluation Material, even though Section 3(a) implies that the Receiving Party is responsible for their Representatives' breaches. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency as described in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Before providing access to Evaluation Material to any Representative, the Receiving Party shall inform such Representative of the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material, and shall advise such Representative that, by accepting possession of or access to such information, such Representative is agreeing to be bound by this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Before providing access to Evaluation Material to any Representative, the Receiving Party shall inform such Representative of the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material, and shall advise such Representative that, by accepting possession of or access to such information, such Representative is agreeing to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.', it creates a contradiction since Evaluation Material is defined in section 2, that any breaches by the Representatives should be taken in account.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used in this Agreement: (i) the term " Receiving Party " means the Party receiving Evaluation Material; (ii) the term " Furnishing Party " means the Party providing Evaluation Material or causing Evaluation Material to be provided; (iii) the term " Representatives " means the directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, investment bankers, financial advisors and other consultants and advisors engaged in connection with the review and evaluation of the Transaction) of the specified Party; and (iv) the term " Evaluation Material " means all proprietary and confidential information concerning the Furnishing Party or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates, whether in verbal, visual, written, electronic or other form, which is made available by the Furnishing Party or any of its Representatives to the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives (" Primary Evaluation Material "), together, in each case, with all notes, memoranda, summaries, analyses, studies, compilations and other writings relating thereto or based in whole or in part thereon prepared by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives (" Derivative Evaluation Material ").
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states 'Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.' The changed text omits this statement. The contradicted text defines 'Representatives' and 'Evaluation Material.' The omission creates a contradiction because, without the responsibility clause, it becomes unclear who is liable for breaches by Representatives concerning Evaluation Material, which is a key aspect of the agreement, thus leading to a direct conflict with the definition provided for Evaluation Material and Representatives.

perturbation.original_text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party is nonetheless, in the opinion of the Receiving Party's or (in the case of disclosure requested or required of a Representative, such Representative's) outside counsel or General Counsel, legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material, the Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material which the Receiving Party is legally required to disclose and, upon the Furnishing Party's request and at the Furnishing Party's expense, shall use all requisite reasonable efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to such Evaluation Material to the extent such assurances are available.
perturbation.changed_text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party is nonetheless, in the opinion of the Receiving Party's outside counsel or General Counsel, legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material, the Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material which the Receiving Party is legally required to disclose and, upon the Furnishing Party's request and at the Furnishing Party's expense, shall use all requisite reasonable efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to such Evaluation Material to the extent such assurances are available.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of '(in the case of disclosure requested or required of a Representative, such Representative's)' creates a contradiction in Section 3(b). The original text allows a Representative's counsel to determine legal compulsion, but the modified text only considers the Receiving Party's counsel. This contradicts the implicit acknowledgment in Section 3(a) that Representatives act on behalf of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Representative's counsel to determine legal compulsion to disclose Evaluation Material. The changed text removes this, limiting the determination only to the Receiving Party's counsel. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, Section 3(a), which states that Evaluation Material will be provided to Representatives to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation, implying that Representatives act on behalf of the Receiving Party. By removing the Representative's counsel's ability to determine legal compulsion, the changed text contradicts the implicit acknowledgment of the Representative's role, thus resulting in an in-text contradiction as defined by Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other Party's Evaluation Material. The Furnishing Party acknowledges that the Receiving Party may currently or in the future develop information internally, or receive information from other parties, that is similar to the Evaluation Material. Accordingly, nothing in this Agreement will be construed as a representation or agreement that the Receiving Party will not develop or have developed for it products, concepts, systems, or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Evaluation Material, provided that 4 the Receiving Party does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to independently develop or acquire products without use of the other Party's Evaluation Material. The Furnishing Party acknowledges that the Receiving Party may currently or in the future develop information internally, or receive information from other parties, that is similar to the Evaluation Material. Accordingly, nothing in this Agreement will be construed as a representation or agreement that the Receiving Party will not develop or have developed for it products, concepts, systems, or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the last clause 'provided that 4 the Receiving Party does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.', it creates a contradiction with the very purpose of the agreement, which is confidentiality of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Evaluation Material (i) shall be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and considering the Transaction; (ii) shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party; and (iii) shall be provided by the Receiving Party solely to those of its Representatives to whom disclosure is reasonably required to facilitate the Receiving Party's evaluation or consideration of the Transaction, it being the intention of the Parties to restrict the dissemination of Evaluation Material to as small a working group as practicable.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a provision that the Receiving Party's independent development is allowed 'provided that the Receiving Party does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.' The changed text removes this provision. The contradicted text states that all Evaluation Material 'shall be kept strictly confidential by the Receiving Party'. By removing the provision in the changed text, the Receiving Party could potentially develop similar products without violating the agreement, even if it used confidential information, thus creating a contradiction with the confidentiality clause.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon receipt of any Third-Party Offer or related inquiry during such period, CIDCO agrees to inform EarthLink of the fact of such Third-Party Offer or related inquiry, including, among other things, the identity of the third-party making such offer or inquiry. Without limiting its application to the entirety of this Agreement, the provisions of Section 7 hereof, including without limitation EarthLink's remedies of specific performance and injunction, shall be specifically applicable to violations of this first paragraph of Section 8. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, nothing contained in this Section 8 shall prohibit the Board of Directors of CIDCO from, only to the minimum extent required, complying with the mandatory disclosure requirements of Rules 14d-9 and 14e-2 promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder with regard to an unsolicited Third-Party Offer, pursuant to advice of outside legal counsel, provided, that any recommendation by the Board of Directors other than to recommend rejection of such offer shall be considered an immediate breach of this Section 8.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately upon receipt of any Third-Party Offer or related inquiry during such period, CIDCO agrees to inform EarthLink of the fact of such Third-Party Offer or related inquiry, including, among other things, the identity of the third-party making such offer or inquiry. Without limiting its application to the entirety of this Agreement, the provisions of Section 7 hereof, including without limitation EarthLink's remedies of specific performance and injunction, shall be specifically applicable to violations of this first paragraph of Section 8. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, nothing contained in this Section 8 shall prohibit the Board of Directors of CIDCO from, only to the minimum extent required, complying with the mandatory disclosure requirements of Rules 14d-9 and 14e-2 promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder with regard to an unsolicited Third-Party Offer, pursuant to advice of outside legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause 'provided, that any recommendation by the Board of Directors other than to recommend rejection of such offer shall be considered an immediate breach of this Section 8.', it creates a contradiction because it implies that the Board could recommend for a offer when the spirit of this agreement is to not do that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CIDCO recognizes EarthLink will incur substantial expenses in conducting a due diligence investigation of CIDCO, and in consideration for EarthLink's doing so, CIDCO agrees to cease and terminate immediately, and to cause its Representatives to cease and terminate immediately, any negotiations (other than with EarthLink and its Representatives) with respect to any proposals relating to any business combination, tender offer or acquisition transaction involving the acquisition by any third party of all or any portion of the stock or assets of CIDCO (any such proposal being a " Third-Party Offer " and any such acquisition being a " Third-Party Acquisition ").
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that any recommendation by the Board of Directors to accept a third-party offer would be a breach. The changed text removes this restriction, implying the Board could recommend acceptance. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states CIDCO agrees to cease negotiations with any third party and implying they won't entertain offers at all. Therefore, the omission creates a strong contradiction.
